name: Sunrise
operator: University of Cambridge / UKAEA / Dell / AMD / StackHPC
oneLiner: A planned £45 million AI supercomputer dedicated to fusion power research and national capability.
summary: Sunrise was announced in 2026 as a £45 million AI supercomputer dedicated to accelerating fusion-energy research. Co-designed by Cambridge Research Computing, UKAEA, Dell, AMD, and StackHPC, it is intended to support fusion plasma modelling and materials science. The project is described as a 1.4 MW GPU-accelerated system that will become one of the UK's largest AI supercomputers and the world's largest dedicated to fusion power, though detailed hardware specifications have not yet been published.

keyFacts.hardware: 1.4 MW GPU-accelerated system; detailed node specifications not yet public
keyFacts.performance: Large-scale national AI service for fusion science; exact AI-exaflops not published
keyFacts.energy: Designed as a dedicated fusion-research AI platform within the UK's national compute expansion

access.route: Planned to integrate into the national AI ecosystem for public-sector fusion, plasma, and materials research.
